structure: rice + 1 kuzhambu + 1 poriyal + 1 kootu + 1 rasam (optional) + curd

nonveg_rule: For nonveg users: replace kuzhambu slot with a nonveg gravy (category=nonveg, name contains kuzhambu) max 3-4 times/week, or add egg dish as poriyal-slot substitute. Fish 2x/week is heart-healthy pattern.

pairing_rules.reason: Spicy-sour kuzhambu needs a mild dal-based side; paruppu thogayal is the classic protein partner
Mild curd gravy pairs with hearty roasted vegetables
Tiffin always served with chutney or sambar
Curd rice traditionally ends the meal (cooling, aids digestion)

avoid_rules.exception: sambar + rasam together is allowed (mildly sour)
avoid_rules.reason: Two strongly tamarind-based dishes clash and over-acidify the meal
Limit saturated fat load; poriyal/kootu coconut garnish doesn't count
Variety across meals; don't suggest cabbage poriyal and cabbage kootu same day

protein_guard.rule: If a generated day's total protein < target (0.83 g per kg body weight, default 50g if weight unknown), the planner MUST upgrade: swap a low-protein poriyal for kootu (adds dal), add paruppu thogayal, choose adai/pongal over plain dosa for tiffin, or add egg for nonveg users.
protein_guard.context: Indian diets are documented protein-deficient (majority of both veg and nonveg households); rice-heavy Tamil meals need deliberate dal/legume pairing. Dal + rice = complete amino acid profile.

daily_nutrition_targets_icmr.note: ICMR-NIN guidelines, sedentary adult defaults; personalize by BMI/goal

diabetic_friendly.note: Favor millets (ragi dosa, ragi kanji), adai; watch white-rice portion

bp_friendly.note: Reduce added salt flag on vatha kuzhambu/pickles; prefer heart_healthy tags

balance_advice_display.per_meal: Show meal card totals: kcal, protein, fiber with 3 status chips: ✅ Balanced / ⚠️ Low protein / ⚠️ Heavy
balance_advice_display.per_day: Day ring chart: kcal vs target, protein vs target, fiber vs target
advice_examples: Today's plan is 12g short on protein — add paruppu thogayal at lunch?
Nice! High-fiber day (32g) — great for digestion.
Two tamarind dishes today — swapped dinner rasam to milagu rasam for balance.
